themes.exampleQuote: I've been to Belgium several times and this place has waffles that are just as good as what you could get in Liège.
I'm an ice cream snob and this is some of the best ice cream I've ever had.
The owner is extremely friendly and nice and helped make the experience that much better.
The comment along the lines of 'I'm not going to waste any more ice cream on you all,' delivered in a rude tone, made us feel uncomfortable.
Add that delicious waffle to it and you go into a brand new realm of delicious.

notable_quote: I've been to Belgium several times and this place has waffles that are just as good as what you could get in Liège.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers return again and again for ice cream they describe in genuinely superlative terms and a Liège waffle they measure against the real thing in Belgium; the one friction point that surfaces across multiple reviews is an inconsistent, occasionally sharp response to sample requests that has cost at least two customers an order.
quarter_narrative: Thirty-two Pittsburgh creators filmed Treat this quarter without any coordination from the business itself, generating over 1.1 million TikTok plays while the shop's own Instagram has been quiet for nearly 200 days. The top video alone, from @dipitcafe, pulled close to 300,000 plays on a caption as spare as 'Waffle with ice cream.' Meanwhile, Page's holds the Pittsburgh Sweets lead this issue on social momentum, exactly the axis where Treat is leaving points on the table. The climb from Ones to Watch toward the upper tier runs directly through closing that gap, either by reactivating the Instagram account that already carries a healthy 7.65 percent engagement rate or by establishing a TikTok presence that meets the creators already showing up.
tldr_read: Over a million TikTok plays from other people's accounts, a dormant Instagram, and a sentiment signal strong enough to anchor the rank. Ones to Watch, #3 in Pittsburgh Ice Cream Shops.
tldr_meaning: The creator layer is already doing the work on TikTok, but without the business posting anything itself, that momentum cannot compound and the Instagram engagement rate sitting at 7.65 percent on a silent account is a stored signal that decays the longer it sits unused.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Third In Ice Cream On Borrowed Momentum. The Shop Has Not Posted In 200 Days.

playbook.headline: Restart Instagram before the signal expires
playbook.action: The account has been silent for 200 days yet still holds a 7.65 percent engagement rate; one post per week recaptures that stored attention before it erodes further.

playbook.headline: Post a sample policy so guests stop guessing
playbook.action: Two separate reviews cite the same sample friction; a clear posted limit, one sentence on a small sign, removes the ambiguity before it appears in a third or fourth review.

playbook.headline: Meet the 32 creators already filming here
playbook.action: With 32 unique creators generating 1.1 million plays and no business TikTok account to link back to, even a minimal presence would give that existing coverage somewhere to land.
